title: Pelexia goyazensis
intro: Pelexia goyazensis is a species of orchid that is native to Brazil. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pelexia goyazensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Pelexia goyazensis include infusions, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, wounds, and digestive issues.

The genus Pelexia is a group of terrestrial orchids native to the Americas, and P. goyazensis is one of its species. The classification of P. goyazensis reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the orchid family.
